pull.title: dbwrapper: avoid copying `CDBIterator` keys in `GetKey()`
pull.body:
### Problem
`CDBIterator::GetKey()` only deserializes the current LevelDB key once and `GetKeyImpl()` already exposes that key as a contiguous borrowed byte span, and `GetKey()` creates a fresh local reader and only performs immediate forward reads before returning.

The copied `DataStream` currently insulates the iterator entry from a failed decode, so switching to a borrowed reader is only safe if a deserialization failure still returns false and leaves the same key/value readable afterward.

> [!NOTE]
> The same simplification does not apply to `GetValue()`, because that path deobfuscates the value bytes in place first and still needs an owning mutable buffer.

### Fix
Add a preparatory test with an invalid reads and checks that the failed decode [does not consume](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/eb85cacd2969caaea682104f498f6b2e6cfb80f8/src/leveldb/include/leveldb/iterator.h#L60-L62) the current iterator entry.
Then switch `GetKey()` to `SpanReader` so the key bytes are read in place instead of being copied into a temporary `DataStream`.

This keeps the same exception swallowing and `bool` return semantics while avoiding the extra allocation and copy.

### Context
Related to https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34483 and https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35025

### Reproducer
`gettxoutsetinfo` is ~10-12% faster for up-to-date blocks (run on SSD), see:

<details><summary>2026-04-20 | gettxoutsetinfo | rpi5-8 | aarch64 | Cortex-A76 | 4 cores | 7.7Gi RAM | ext4 | SSD</summary>

```
COMMITS="64a88c8c1edc7ee5cef623d9aa8179a239e27ce9 57dc0202ddb7b4cbdd521fb237a25fc4d7f28ddf"; \
BASE_DIR="/mnt/my_storage"; DATA_DIR="$BASE_DIR/BitcoinData"; LOG_DIR="$BASE_DIR/logs"; \
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" && \
(echo ""; for c in $COMMITS; do git cat-file -e "$c^{commit}" 2>/dev/null || git fetch -q origin "$c" || exit 1; git log -1 --pretty='%h %s' "$c" || exit 1; done) && \
(echo "" && echo "$(date -I) | gettxoutsetinfo | $(hostname) | $(uname -m) | $(lscpu | grep 'Model name' | head -1 | cut -d: -f2 | xargs) | $(nproc) cores | $(free -h | awk '/^Mem:/{print $2}') RAM | $(df -T $BASE_DIR | awk 'NR==2{print $2}') | $(lsblk -no ROTA $(df --output=source $BASE_DIR | tail -1) | grep -q 1 && echo HDD || echo SSD)"; echo "") && \
hyperfine \
  --sort command \
  --runs 10 \
  --export-json "$BASE_DIR/gettxoutsetinfo-$(sed -E 's/([a-f0-9]{8})[a-f0-9]* ?/\1-/g;s/-$//'<<<"$COMMITS")-$(date +%s).json" \
  --parameter-list COMMIT ${COMMITS// /,} \
  --prepare "killall -9 bitcoind 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f $DATA_DIR/debug.log; git clean -fxd && git reset --hard {COMMIT} && \
    cmake -B build -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release && ninja -C build bitcoind bitcoin-cli -j$(nproc) && \
    ./build/bin/bitcoind -datadir=$DATA_DIR -connect=0 -listen=0 -dnsseed=0 -coinstatsindex=0 -txindex=0 -blockfilterindex=0 -daemon -printtoconsole=0; \
    ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=$DATA_DIR -rpcwait getblockcount >/dev/null" \
  --conclude "./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=$DATA_DIR stop 2>/dev/null || true; killall bitcoind 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 10; \
    grep -q 'Done loading' $DATA_DIR/debug.log && grep 'Bitcoin Core version' $DATA_DIR/debug.log | grep -q \"\$(git rev-parse --short=12 {COMMIT})\"; \
    cp $DATA_DIR/debug.log $LOG_DIR/gettxoutsetinfo-{COMMIT}-$(date +%s).log" \
  "./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=$DATA_DIR -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null"

64a88c8c1e Merge bitcoin/bitcoin#35096: kernel: align height parameters to int32_t in btck API
57dc0202dd dbwrapper: use SpanReader for iterator keys

Benchmark 1: ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = 64a88c8c1edc7ee5cef623d9aa8179a239e27ce9)
  Time (mean ± σ):     109.002 s ±  3.091 s    [User: 0.003 s, System: 0.004 s]
  Range (min … max):   106.191 s … 113.608 s    10 runs

Benchmark 2: ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = 57dc0202ddb7b4cbdd521fb237a25fc4d7f28ddf)
  Time (mean ± σ):     97.711 s ±  1.172 s    [User: 0.003 s, System: 0.004 s]
  Range (min … max):   96.651 s … 100.104 s    10 runs

Relative speed comparison
        1.12 ±  0.03  ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = 64a88c8c1edc7ee5cef623d9aa8179a239e27ce9)
        1.00          ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = 57dc0202ddb7b4cbdd521fb237a25fc4d7f28ddf)
```

</details>

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Concept ACK | [andrewtoth](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35128#issuecomment-4297979905) | | Stale ACK | [sedited](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35128#pullrequestreview-4147759365) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.

ACK 5de2f97a0521fe75b47f9840141c4ace74656de9

Saw a ~7% performance boost on `gettxoutsetinfo` on my i9.

> I’d prefer to do it in a follow-up PR 

I don't think it depends on this, so please make a PR and I will ACK!

> There are a few similar DataStream reuse opportunities

I wasn't able to find any others. Other places with one-shot `DataStream` usages would not work due to being `const` and/or not being thread-safe. `CDBIterator` must only be accessed in a single thread. This seems like the only place where this would be a benefit. Maybe `Seek` but that is only called once, and can reuse the same one in `CDBIterator`. Did I miss anywhere?

> make sure oversized values don’t leave large allocations hanging around longer than intended

The usages are `Coin`, `CDiskBlockIndex`, compact block filters. None of these are very large. Also, all usages of `CDBIterator` are wrapped in a local `std::unique_ptr` which is then destroyed after it is iterated and goes out of scope.

tree 2e94498173fd965d9608f973a787079d312300ca
parent cd7865b0ce17568945c7f72db0248489cf284787
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776846406 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929342 +0200

streams: add `ScopedDataStreamUsage`

Some code paths reuse `DataStream` scratch buffers across calls or loop iterations.

Add a small scope helper that asserts a scratch stream is empty on entry, clears it on scope exit, and drops unusually large retained allocations above a default threshold instead of keeping them around for the owner lifetime.

Add direct unit coverage for the helper in `streams_tests`, checking that retained capacities stay unchanged across scope exit while oversized retained capacities are dropped.

events.message:

Add direct unit coverage for the helper in `streams_tests`, checking that retained capacities stay unchanged across scope exit while oversized retained capacities are dropped.

author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776851284 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929342 +0200

test: cover repeated dbwrapper stream use

The dbwrapper changes are easiest to validate by repeating operations on the same owning object and making sure the public behavior stays stable across success and failure paths.

Extend the existing dbwrapper tests to reuse a `CDBBatch` after `Clear()`, seek multiple times on the same iterator, and repeat `Read()`, `Exists()`, and `EstimateSize()` calls on the same `CDBWrapper`.

tree fd7520f5722a3099740b2db3766a2bf749143280
parent 0f4edc1d2c215920af00063370d42ff4e9a58b49
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776846430 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929347 +0200

dbwrapper: guard `CDBBatch` scratch streams

`CDBBatch` already keeps key and value serialization buffers as reusable members.

Wrap each `Write()` and `Erase()` use in `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so the buffers must start empty and are cleared on every exit path.

This also makes it safe for `Clear()` to assert that the scratch streams are already clean instead of repairing them itself, because `Clear()` only resets the underlying LevelDB write batch and callers such as `CCoinsViewDB::BatchWrite()` invoke it after those guarded helpers have returned.

events.message:

`CDBBatch` already keeps key and value serialization buffers as reusable members.

Wrap each `Write()` and `Erase()` use in `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so the buffers must start empty and are cleared on every exit path.

This also makes it safe for `Clear()` to assert that the scratch streams are already clean instead of repairing them itself, because `Clear()` only resets the underlying LevelDB write batch and callers such as `CCoinsViewDB::BatchWrite()` invoke it after those guarded helpers have returned.

tree b63b2be487717c68e5d0fb61b92b6abbe0d40d50
parent 8f01a65fc3c787be1a3b8e2e0871afa62d8b40d5
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776846450 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929350 +0200

dbwrapper: reuse iterator seek scratch stream

`CDBIterator::Seek()` serializes its lookup key only to hand it straight to `SeekImpl()`.

Keep that serialization buffer on the iterator and guard it with `ScopedDataStreamUsage` instead of rebuilding a fresh `DataStream` on every call.

This is safe because `SeekImpl()` only needs the key bytes for the immediate LevelDB seek call and does not retain a pointer to the scratch buffer after it returns.

tree 3e374a95e9f733247e8610b5ae26a130313d3218
parent 18bcdf35c1f1a9f8e40a1a4212853879cf9055d7
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776846476 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929350 +0200

dbwrapper: keep read-side key streams local

`CDBWrapper::Read()`, `Exists()`, and `EstimateSize()` serialize keys only for immediate LevelDB calls, but unlike `CDBBatch` and `CDBIterator`, these const methods may be called concurrently on the same wrapper.

Keep their key streams as local temporaries and document that choice instead of moving them onto `CDBWrapper` itself.

test: cover repeated wallet batch stream use

The upcoming `DatabaseBatch` scratch-buffer refactor reuses the same key and value streams across multiple operations on one batch object.

Fold the reuse checks into the existing write-error test so it also exercises mixed success and failure `Read()`, `Exists()`, `Write()`, and `Erase()` calls on the same batch before the refactor lands.

tree 19b0399c7512e6b3ca1f33f622853cbcee60848b
parent 80bb30ccbe87ada6758fb6007e0248d1cf136623
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776846503 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929350 +0200

walletdb: reuse batch scratch streams

`DatabaseBatch::Read()`, `Write()`, `Erase()`, and `Exists()` still serialized keys and values into fresh local `DataStream`s on every call.

Move those staging buffers onto `DatabaseBatch` and guard them with `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so repeated batch operations can reuse the same allocations.

This is safe because `DatabaseBatch` is already a stateful handle, the scratch bytes are only used for the immediate backend call, and the scope guard clears the moved-from buffers before control returns so the next call still starts from a clean state.

events.message:

Move those staging buffers onto `DatabaseBatch` and guard them with `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so repeated batch operations can reuse the same allocations.

This is safe because `DatabaseBatch` is already a stateful handle, the scratch bytes are only used for the immediate backend call, and the scope guard clears the moved-from buffers before control returns so the next call still starts from a clean state.

tree c006e489656d034d98f72a2d75953a80685592af
parent 292b1b21da9165ffc790c77e1380fe27ecb791e2
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776846492 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1776929350 +0200

walletdb: make cursor `Next()` overwrite streams

`BerkeleyROCursor::Next()` appended into the caller-provided key and value streams, unlike the existing SQLite cursor implementation which overwrote them.

Add a Berkeley RO reproducer to the existing prefix-cursor test by constructing a read-only database in memory, preloading its records, prefilling the output streams with junk, and checking that `Next()` returns only the record contents.

Fix the bug by clearing the Berkeley RO output streams before writing them, and keep the higher-level wallet loading code on the simpler cursor-overwrite contract.

events.message:
walletdb: make cursor `Next()` overwrite streams

`BerkeleyROCursor::Next()` appended into the caller-provided key and value streams, unlike the existing SQLite cursor implementation which overwrote them.

Add a Berkeley RO reproducer to the existing prefix-cursor test by constructing a read-only database in memory, preloading its records, prefilling the output streams with junk, and checking that `Next()` returns only the record contents.

Fix the bug by clearing the Berkeley RO output streams before writing them, and keep the higher-level wallet loading code on the simpler cursor-overwrite contract.
